revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:中臣大島]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Princess Nukata|qid=Q119461|1=Princess Nukata}}|father={{ill|Q108045133|qid=Q108045133|1=Nakatomi no Kome}}}}
{{Infobox noble | name = Nakatomi no Ōshima | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = 11th day of the 3rd lunar month of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s 7th year (April 21, 693) | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = Chokutai-ni, {{ill|Jingihaku|ja|神祇伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingihaku|1=Minister of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17210025}} | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} → {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} (Nakatomi no Muraji → Nakatomi no Ason → Fujiwara no Ason → Nakatomi no Ason) | parents = Father: Nakatomi no Komi (渠毎) | siblings = Adachi (a student sent to Tang China) | spouse = | children = Mayoi }}
'''Nakatomi no Ōshima''' (中臣 大島) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|Shi (poetry)|en|Shi (poetry)|qid=Q1759242|lt=Chinese poet|1=Shi (poetry)}} active during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. Initially bearing the {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}} Rank in the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}, he later adopted the titles Nakatomi no Ason, Fujiwara no Ason, and returned to Nakatomi no Ason. He was the son of Nakatomi no Komi and the nephew of {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11366917}}. His court Rank was Chokutai-ni, and he held the position of {{ill|Jingihaku|ja|神祇伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingihaku|1=Minister of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17210025}}.
== Career ==
Following the legacy of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|qid=Q433995|lt=Nakatomi no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}} and Nakatomi no Kane, Ōshima served as the head of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} and became a central figure in both domestic and diplomatic affairs during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress Jitō. His role in state rituals and governance was significant during the formative period of the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|qid=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Traditional Chinese law}} system.<ref name="anr">Morikimi Kōshō, "Nakatomi no Ōshima," in the {{ill|Asahi Nihon Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten|ja|朝日日本歴史人物事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Nihon Rekishi Jinbutsu Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
In 681 CE (10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign), he was appointed alongside {{ill|Prince Kawashima|ja|川島皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kawashima|qq=}}, {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|qid=Q3403344|lt=Prince Takechi|1=Prince Osakabe}}, and others to compile and verify historical texts such as the {{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|qid=Q1107261|lt=Teiki|1=Teiki}} and the {{ill|Honji|ja|本辞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ōshima, along with {{ill|Heguri no Koobito|ja|平群子首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Koobito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, took an active role in writing these records. At the time, he held the Rank of Daisanjo (大山上).<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu10th year 3月17日条</ref> Later that year, he was promoted to Shōkonge (小錦下).
In 683 CE, Ōshima participated in a mission to delineate provincial borders with officials like {{ill|Prince Ise|ja|伊勢王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ise|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hatada no Yakuni|ja|羽田矢国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatada no Yakuni|1=Hata no Yakuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610319}}, although the task was incomplete.<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu12nd year 12月13日条</ref> Following the enactment of the {{ill|Eight Ranks of Nobility|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Ranks of Nobility|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} in 684 CE, he changed his Rank from Muraji to Ason and later to Fujiwara no Ason. However, he eventually reverted to Nakatomi no Ason.
In 686 CE, he was dispatched to {{ill|Tsukushi|en|Tsukushi|qid=Q422471|lt=Chikushi|1=Tsukushi}} alongside {{ill|Kawachi-no-Mikoto|ja|河内王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi no Ōkimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Anamaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Anamaro|1=Otomo no Otomaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11432337}}, {{ill|Sakaibe no Kajiura|ja|境部鯯魚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Kajiura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Hozumi no Muchimaro|ja|穂積虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Muchimaro|1=Hozumi Mushimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129398}} to receive {{ill|Silla envoys|ja|新羅使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla envoys|1=Silla envoy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55534500}}.<ref>『日本書紀』朱鳥元年正月条</ref> In 690 CE, during Empress Jitō's enthronement ceremony, Ōshima served as {{ill|Jingihaku|ja|神祇伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingihaku|1=Minister of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17210025}}, delivering a ceremonial address.<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇4th year 正月1日条</ref>
He passed away on the 11th day of the 3rd lunar month of 693 CE.
== Legacy ==
As a {{ill|Shi (poetry)|en|Shi (poetry)|qid=Q1759242|lt=Chinese poet|1=Shi (poetry)}}, two of his works are included in the {{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|qid=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}} anthology. He also contributed to Buddhism, such as initiating the construction of the {{ill|Awabaraji Temple|ja|粟原寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awabaraji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 粟原寺跡|1=Ōbara-dera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604562}} for {{ill|Prince Kusakabe|en|Prince Kusakabe|qid=Q1046438|lt=Prince Kusakabe|1=Prince Kusakabe}}.<ref>Inscription on the bronze lotus pedestal of Awabaraji's three-story pagoda.</ref>
== Wife: Hime no Ason Nukada ==
The inscription on the {{ill|Tanzan Shrine|lt=bronze lotus pedestal of Awabaraji's pagoda|ja|談山神社#国宝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanzan Shrine|qq=}} indicates that Ōshima's wife, Hime no Ason Nukada, lived nearly 80 years. Scholars like {{ill|Okabe Itsuko|ja|岡部伊都子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okabe Itsuko|1=Itsuko Okabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11473814}} and {{ill|Takeshi Umehara|en|Takeshi Umehara|qid=Q3818175|lt=Umehara Takeshi|1=Takeshi Umehara}} have theorized that she may be identical to {{ill|Princess Nukata|en|Princess Nukata|qid=Q119461|lt=Princess Nukada|1=Princess Nukata}}, though this remains debated.
== Career Timeline ==
* 681 CE: Appointed Daisanjo; promoted to Shōkonge. * 684 CE: Name changed from Muraji to Ason under the Eight Ranks of Nobility. * 686 CE: Participated in Silla envoy reception as Chokutai-shi. * 690 CE: Served as {{ill|Jingihaku|ja|神祇伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingihaku|1=Minister of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17210025}} during Empress Jitō's enthronement. * 693 CE: Passed away as Chokutai-ni.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author={{ill|Ujitani Makoto|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Makoto|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449517}}|title=日本書紀（下）|publisher=講談社学術文庫|year=1988}}
{{translated page|ja|中臣大島|version=89651948|comment=Imported full ja history}}
